title: 9 Essential Herbal Creams for Controlling High Blood Pressure

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Valerenic acid is good for high blood pressure because it helps to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, which in turn lowers blood pressure by reducing the amount of pressure on blood vessel walls.
Valeranone is good for high blood pressure because it helps to relax blood vessels, which reduces blood pressure and prevents damage to the heart and other organs.
Valerenol is good for high blood pressure because it helps lower blood pressure by relaxing blood vessels and reducing stress on the cardiovascular system.

Tannins, also known as lemon balm, are good for high blood pressure because they can help relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, which in turn lowers blood pressure by reducing the amount of strain on the heart.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian root, is a natural herb that has been used for centuries to help lower blood pressure. When applied topically as a cream, it's believed to work in several ways. Firstly, valerian root cream may help relax the blood vessels, allowing them to widen and improve blood flow. This is because valerian root contains compounds like valerenic acid, which have a calming effect on the nervous system. By reducing stress and anxiety, the cream may help lower blood pressure levels. Additionally, valerian root cream may also help improve sleep quality, as it's often used as a natural sedative. Poor sleep can contribute to high blood pressure, so addressing this underlying issue can help alleviate the condition. When applied directly to the skin, the cream's active compounds may also help reduce inflammation and improve circulation, further contributing to lower blood pressure. Regular use of valerian root cream may lead to noticeable improvements in blood pressure readings over time.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Melissa officinalis herbal creams that help with high blood pressure, you will need Melissa officinalis (also known as lemon balm) leaves, a carrier oil like coconut oil or olive oil, beeswax, shea butter, vitamin E oil, and a double boiler. First, dry the Melissa officinalis leaves in a low-temperature oven or by air-drying them for a few days. Then, combine the dried leaves with the carrier oil in a clean glass jar and let it steep in a cool, dark place for about two weeks, shaking the jar daily. After the steeping process, strain the liquid and discard the solids. Next, melt the beeswax and shea butter in the double boiler, and then add the liquid from the Melissa officinalis steeping process, vitamin E oil, and a few drops of your preferred essential oil (like lavender or chamomile). Continue to heat the mixture until it reaches 120-130°F, then remove it from the heat and let it cool. Once the mixture has cooled and thickened, pour it into a clean container and let it cool completely before using the Melissa officinalis herbal cream to help lower blood pressure and promote relaxation.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal creams for high blood pressure

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Polyphenols are good for high blood pressure because they help relax blood vessels, which can lower blood pressure and improve heart health.

Tannins, also known as Lemon Balm, are good for high blood pressure because they help to relax blood vessels and improve circulation, thereby reducing blood pressure and promoting overall cardiovascular health.
Quercetin is good for high blood pressure because it helps relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, which lowers blood pressure and prevents damage to the heart and blood vessels.
Kaempferol is good for high blood pressure because it helps relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, which can lower blood pressure and reduce the risk of heart disease.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata cream is made from the herb passionflower, which has been used for centuries to help calm the body and mind. High blood pressure is often caused by stress and anxiety, which can increase the force of the blood against the walls of the blood vessels. The passionflower in the cream helps to calm the nervous system, reducing feelings of stress and anxiety. This, in turn, helps to relax the blood vessels, allowing them to widen and reduce blood pressure. Additionally, passionflower has been shown to have antioxidant properties, which help to protect the blood vessels from damage and inflammation. By reducing stress and protecting the blood vessels, Passiflora incarnata cream may help to lower blood pressure and improve overall cardiovascular health.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Melissa officinalis herbal creams for high blood pressure, you will need fresh Melissa officinalis leaves, a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl. First, dry the Melissa officinalis leaves in a warm, dry place or by using a food dehydrator at a low temperature. Once the leaves are dry, chop them into small pieces and combine them with the carrier oil in a clean glass jar. Let the mixture steep in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day, to allow the herbs to infuse into the oil. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl, discarding the solids. Next, melt the beeswax in the double boiler or microwave it in short increments, stirring between each heating. Once the beeswax is melted, slowly pour it into the infused oil, stirring constantly to prevent separation. Continue stirring until the mixture has cooled and thickened, then transfer it to a clean container and store it in the refrigerator. To use the cream, simply apply a small amount to the skin and massage it in, taking care not to stretch or pull the skin. Regular use of this cream may help to lower blood pressure, but consult with a healthcare professional before making any changes to your treatment plan.
remedies.image_alt: Passiflora incarnata herbal creams for high blood pressure

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin (Lemon Balm) is good for high blood pressure because it helps to relax blood vessels, allowing blood to flow more easily and reducing blood pressure.
Kaempferol is good for high blood pressure because it helps to relax and widen blood vessels, making it easier for blood to flow and reducing pressure in the blood vessels.
Isorhapontigenin is good for high blood pressure because it helps to relax blood vessels, allowing blood to flow more smoothly and reducing the pressure in the blood vessels.
Bilobalide is good for high blood pressure because it helps to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, reducing the force of blood on artery walls and lowering blood pressure.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams are thought to be good for high blood pressure because they help relax blood vessels. The cream contains a special extract from the Ginkgo biloba plant, which widens blood vessels and improves blood flow. This makes it easier for the heart to pump blood, reducing the pressure inside the blood vessels. As a result, blood pressure may decrease. Ginkgo biloba creams may also reduce inflammation in blood vessels, which can contribute to high blood pressure. Furthermore, they may help reduce the amount of stress hormones in the body, such as adrenaline, which can increase blood pressure. Some people believe that Ginkgo biloba creams can improve the health of blood vessels over time, making it easier for them to relax and reduce blood pressure.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Melissa officinalis herbal cream that helps with high blood pressure, you will need Melissa officinalis flowers, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a preservative. First, dry the Melissa officinalis flowers completely to prevent mold and bacteria growth. Then, make a herbal infusion by steeping a teaspoon of dried flowers in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Next, let the mixture cool and strain it into a bowl. Add a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil to the cooled herbal infusion in a ratio of 1:5, so if you have 100ml of herbal infusion, you will add 500ml of carrier oil. Mix well and heat the mixture gently until the beeswax is fully incorporated. Beeswax is a natural emulsifier that will help to thicken the cream and protect it from oxidation. Once the mixture is smooth and creamy, add a preservative to prevent bacteria and mold growth. Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool completely before use. This Melissa officinalis herbal cream can be applied topically to the skin to help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common causes of high blood pressure. However, it is essential to note that while Melissa officinalis has been traditionally used to help with high blood pressure, it should not be used as a replacement for medical treatment. Consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal creams for high blood pressure

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Linalool (also known as lemon balm) is good for high blood pressure because it has been shown to help relax blood vessels and lower blood pressure by reducing stress and anxiety.

Linalyl acetate (lemon balm) is good for high blood pressure because it helps to relax blood vessels, lower stress and anxiety, and improve sleep quality, which can all contribute to reducing blood pressure levels.
Rosmarinic acid is good for high blood pressure because it helps lower blood pressure by relaxing blood vessels and improving blood flow, making it easier for the heart to pump blood.
Lavandulol is good for high blood pressure because it helps to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, which can lower blood pressure and reduce the risk of heart disease.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, has been used for many years to help lower blood pressure. The main reason for this is that it contains a group of compounds called flavonoids, which have been shown to relax blood vessels and reduce the heart's workload. When blood vessels relax, it becomes easier for the heart to pump blood, which in turn can lower blood pressure. This is especially helpful for people with high blood pressure, as it can reduce the risk of heart disease and strokes. Additionally, Lavandula angustifolia creams may also help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common causes of high blood pressure. The calming effect of the cream can help slow down the heart rate and promote a sense of relaxation, further contributing to lower blood pressure.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Melissa officinalis herbal creams that help with high blood pressure, you'll need to start by harvesting the leaves of the lemon balm plant, Melissa officinalis, and drying them in a cool, dark place. Once the leaves are dry, you can infuse them in a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil by placing the leaves in a clean glass jar and covering them with the oil. Let the mixture sit for 2-3 weeks in a cool, dark place, shaking the jar every few days, until the oil has taken on the flavor and scent of the lemon balm. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, and discard the solids. Next, you can mix the infused oil with a base cream, such as shea butter or beeswax, and add a few drops of vitamin E oil to help preserve the cream and protect your skin. Finally, store the cream in a cool, dark place and apply it to your skin as needed to help reduce high blood pressure and promote overall well-being.

remedies.remedy_desc: Crataegus monogyna cream, also known as hawthorn cream, is sometimes used to help lower high blood pressure. This is because hawthorn is a plant that has been used in medicine for a long time. It is said to have properties that can help relax blood vessels and make it easier for the heart to pump blood. The cream is made from the leaves and flowers of the hawthorn plant, which are rich in flavonoids and other active compounds. When applied to the skin, these compounds can be absorbed into the bloodstream, where they may help to improve blood flow and lower blood pressure. Some people use hawthorn cream as a natural way to manage high blood pressure, but it's essential to talk to a doctor before trying it, as they can interact with other medications and have side effects in some individuals. The cream can also help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common causes of high blood pressure. The flavonoids in hawthorn cream may also help protect the heart and blood vessels from damage caused by high blood pressure.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Melissa officinalis herbal creams that help with high blood pressure, start by gathering dried Melissa leaves and a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil. Combine 2 tablespoons of dried Melissa leaves with 1 cup of carrier oil in a clean glass jar and let it steep for 2-3 weeks in a cool, dark place, shaking the jar occasionally. After the infusion is complete, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Next, add a few drops of vitamin E oil to the infused oil to help preserve it and prevent spoilage. Then, mix 1/4 cup of the infused oil with 1/4 cup of a thickening agent like beeswax or shea butter to create a smooth, spreadable cream. Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in short intervals, stirring well between each interval, until the beeswax or shea butter has melted and the mixture is smooth. Finally, pour the cream into a clean glass jar and let it cool completely before using it to help lower high blood pressure by reducing stress and anxiety, and improving sleep quality.

Isorhapontigenin, also known as lemon balm, is good for high blood pressure because it helps to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, which can help to lower blood pressure and reduce the risk of heart disease.

Triterpenoids, also known as lemon balm, are good for high blood pressure because they have been found to help relax blood vessels and reduce blood pressure by inhibiting the enzyme ACE, which constricts blood vessels.

remedies.remedy_desc: Astragalus membranaceus cream is a natural remedy that may help lower high blood pressure. The cream is made from the roots of the Astragalus plant, which has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries. It is believed to help lower blood pressure by relaxing the blood vessels and improving blood flow. This is because the cream contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce the swelling of blood vessels and improve their flexibility. Additionally, Astragalus membranaceus cream may also help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for high blood pressure. By reducing stress and promoting relaxation, the cream can help to lower blood pressure and improve overall cardiovascular health. The cream may also help to improve kidney function, which is important for removing excess fluids and salt from the body, both of which can contribute to high blood pressure. Some studies have suggested that Astragalus membranaceus cream may be effective in lowering blood pressure in people with mild to moderate hypertension.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Melissa officinalis herbal creams that help with high blood pressure, first, you need to prepare the herbal extract. Start by gathering dried Melissa officinalis leaves, which can be bought from a local health store or online. Next, combine 2 tablespoons of dried Melissa leaves with 1 cup of boiling water in a glass bowl. Let it steep for 10-15 minutes, then strain the liquid and let it cool. After that, mix the cooled liquid with 1/2 cup of carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a small bowl. Then, you need to add a thickening agent like beeswax or shea butter to the mixture to give it a cream-like texture. Melt the beeswax or shea butter in a double boiler or in the microwave for a few seconds, then slowly add it to the oil and Melissa extract mixture, stirring constantly until it's well combined. Finally, pour the mixture into a glass jar and store it in the fridge to use as needed. When applying the cream to the skin, gently massage it in and leave it on for at least 30 minutes to an hour before showering or bathing. This herbal cream can help to lower blood pressure by relaxing the blood vessels and reducing stress.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis, also known as rosemary, is a popular herb used in creams for its medicinal benefits. The active compound in rosemary, called carnosic acid, is known to help lower blood pressure in people with hypertension. When applied topically in a cream form, rosemary can help relax blood vessels, allowing blood to flow more easily and reducing pressure inside the blood vessels. This can be particularly beneficial for people who have high blood pressure due to stress or anxiety, as the aroma of rosemary can also help to calm the mind and body. Additionally, rosemary cream may also help to improve circulation and reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, further contributing to its potential benefits for people with high blood pressure.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Melissa officinalis herbal creams that help with high blood pressure, you will need some dried Melissa officinalis leaves, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a double boiler or a heat-proof glass bowl set over a pot of simmering water. First, combine the dried Melissa officinalis leaves with the carrier oil in a small saucepan, and gently heat it over low heat for about 30 minutes to allow the herbs to infuse their properties into the oil. Next, strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate container, and discard the solids. In a separate saucepan, melt the beeswax over low heat, then slowly add the infused oil to the melted beeswax while continuously stirring until well combined. Remove the saucepan from the heat and pour the mixture into a clean glass jar, allowing it to cool and solidify into a creamy texture. Once set, your Melissa officinalis herbal cream is ready to use, and can be applied topically to the skin to help reduce blood pressure and promote relaxation.

Ginsenosides are good for high blood pressure because they help relax blood vessels, which allows blood to flow more easily and reduces the pressure inside the blood vessels.
Flavonoids are good for high blood pressure because they help relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, which reduces the pressure inside the blood vessels and keeps blood pressure under control.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng creams may help with high blood pressure because it has compounds that can relax blood vessels. When blood vessels relax, they don't get as narrow, which means blood can flow more easily. This can lower blood pressure. Panax ginseng creams also have antioxidants that can protect the body from damage caused by free radicals. Free radicals can cause blood vessels to get damaged, making them more likely to get narrow. By protecting the body from free radicals, Panax ginseng creams may help prevent this damage. Some studies have shown that Panax ginseng can also help reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to high blood pressure. When we're stressed or anxious, our blood vessels can get narrower, which can increase blood pressure. By reducing stress and anxiety, Panax ginseng creams may help lower blood pressure.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Melissa officinalis herbal creams that help with high blood pressure, first, you will need to harvest and dry the Melissa officinalis leaves. You can do this by cutting the leaves from the plant in the summer months when they are at their peak potency, and then drying them in a warm, dark place or using a food dehydrator. Once the leaves are dry, you can crush them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle. Next, you will need to make a herbal infusion by mixing the dried Melissa officinalis powder with a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a glass jar. Let the mixture sit for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily, to allow the herbs to infuse into the oil. After the infusion is complete, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another glass jar, and discard the solids. Finally, you can add the herbal infusion to a cream base, such as shea butter or beeswax, and mix well to create a rich and soothing cream that can be applied to the skin to help lower high blood pressure.

Kaempferol is good for high blood pressure because it helps to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, which lowers blood pressure and reduces the risk of heart disease.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, is a plant that has been used for centuries to help with high blood pressure. The cream made from this plant is good for high blood pressure because it helps to relax the blood vessels. When the blood vessels are relaxed, they get wider, which allows the blood to flow more easily and reduces the pressure inside the blood vessels. This helps to lower the blood pressure. The cream also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling in the blood vessels. This swelling can cause the blood pressure to rise, so by reducing it, the cream can help to keep the blood pressure under control. Additionally, the cream can help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common causes of high blood pressure. When we are stressed, our blood vessels constrict, which increases the blood pressure. So, by reducing stress, the cream can help to lower the blood pressure and keep it at a healthy level.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Melissa officinalis herbal creams that help with high blood pressure, you will need some fresh Melissa leaves, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, a beeswax, and a double boiler. First, dry the Melissa leaves by tying them in small bunches and hanging them upside down in a warm, dry place. Once the leaves are dry, chop them into small pieces and add them to a saucepan with the carrier oil. Heat the mixture over low heat for a few hours, stirring occasionally, until the oil has turned a light green color and the mixture has cooled down. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl, discarding the solids. Next, melt the beeswax in the double boiler and add the infused oil to it. Stir until the mixture has thickened and turned into a creamy consistency. Let the cream cool down and then transfer it to a jar. Apply the cream to your skin as needed, ideally after a warm bath or shower, to help lower high blood pressure naturally.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal creams for high blood pressure

intro_desc: High blood pressure is a serious health issue that can lead to heart disease, stroke, and kidney damage if left unchecked. It's essential to manage it to prevent these complications. Herbal creams can be a useful addition to your treatment plan, as they often contain natural ingredients that can help lower blood pressure.  Herbs like Valeriana officinalis (valerian root) and Passiflora incarnata (passionflower) are particularly beneficial. Valerian root has been shown to help reduce anxiety and stress, which are common contributors to high blood pressure. When we're stressed, our blood vessels constrict, making it harder for blood to flow. Valerian root's calming effects can help relax the blood vessels, allowing blood to flow more easily and reducing blood pressure. Passionflower, on the other hand, has a sedative effect, which can also help calm the nervous system and promote relaxation, further contributing to lower blood pressure.  Using herbal creams that contain these herbs can be an effective way to manage high blood pressure. By applying these creams to the skin, the active ingredients are absorbed and can help to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation, leading to lower blood pressure. It's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies to ensure they won't interact with any medications or exacerbate underlying conditions.
supplementary_best: A combination of herbal creams containing Crataegus monogyna, Panax ginseng, and Lavandula angustifolia may help remedy high blood pressure. Crataegus monogyna, also known as hawthorn, is a herb that helps to reduce blood pressure by relaxing blood vessel walls, improving blood flow, and strengthening heart function. Panax ginseng, or Asian ginseng, can also help lower blood pressure by reducing stress and anxiety, promoting relaxation, and improving cardiovascular health. Lavandula angustifolia, or English lavender, has a calming effect on the body, reducing stress and anxiety, which can contribute to high blood pressure.  This combination is effective because it addresses multiple factors that can contribute to high blood pressure, such as stress and anxiety, blood vessel constriction, and heart function. The anti-inflammatory properties of Panax ginseng may also help to reduce inflammation in blood vessels, which can further contribute to high blood pressure. By combining these three herbs, individuals may be able to lower their blood pressure and improve their cardiovascular health.
supplementary_avoid: If you have high blood pressure, there are some herbal creams you should avoid. One of them is Yohimbe, which can raise blood pressure even more. This is because it has a similar effect to certain medications used to treat high blood pressure. Another one is Ephedra, which can also increase blood pressure due to its stimulating properties. This can be a problem if you're already struggling to keep your blood pressure under control. Yohimbe and Ephedra can interact with medications you're taking, making things worse.  Another herbal cream to be careful with is Ma Huang (also known as Ephedra Vulgaris), which is similar to Ephedra. It's a stimulant that can raise blood pressure and interact with medications.  Also, beware of Ginger, especially if you're taking medications that lower blood pressure. Ginger can have a blood-thinning effect, which may not be good if you're taking medications that do the same thing. This can increase the risk of bleeding.  Finally, avoid using Creams with high levels of Caffeine or Guarana, as they can also raise blood pressure due to their stimulating properties. If you must use these creams, be sure to check with your doctor first to make sure it's safe for you.
supplementary_other_remedies: Other natural remedies that can help with high blood pressure include omega-3 fatty acids found in fish like salmon and sardines. These healthy fats help to lower blood pressure by reducing inflammation and improving blood vessel function. Inflammation is a known cause of high blood pressure, so by reducing it, omega-3s help to lower blood pressure naturally.  Another natural remedy is magnesium, an essential mineral that helps to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow. Many people are deficient in magnesium, which can lead to high blood pressure. Magnesium supplements or foods rich in magnesium like dark leafy greens and nuts can help to lower blood pressure.  Potassium-rich foods like bananas, avocados, and sweet potatoes can also help to lower blood pressure. Potassium helps to balance out the effects of sodium and can reduce blood pressure by relaxing blood vessels. Regular exercise, such as walking or yoga, can also help to lower blood pressure by improving blood flow and reducing stress.  Additionally, reducing sodium intake can help to lower blood pressure. The American Heart Association recommends limiting sodium intake to less than 2,300 milligrams per day. Foods high in sodium like processed meats and canned goods should be avoided or consumed in moderation.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with high blood pressure are more likely to experience heart problems, like angina (pain in the chest) and heart failure (when the heart can't pump enough blood). They are also at risk of having a stroke (when the blood supply to the brain is cut off) and kidney damage (when the kidneys can't filter waste properly). Some may develop peripheral artery disease (when the arteries in the legs and arms get narrower), causing pain and weakness in the legs.  Certain medicinal herbs can help with these conditions. For example, Hawthorn is a heart-friendly herb that helps to open up narrowed blood vessels and improve blood flow to the heart. It also reduces blood pressure and strengthens the heart muscle. Turmeric, with its active compound Curcumin, helps to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, making them less likely to become narrowed. Ginkgo Biloba improves blood flow to the brain, which can help to prevent strokes. Dandelion root and Milk Thistle help to clean the blood and reduce pressure on the kidneys, which can help to prevent kidney damage.  Garlic has been shown to lower blood pressure and reduce the risk of heart problems by helping to open up narrowed blood vessels. Ginger also has a positive effect on blood pressure and can help to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels. Olive leaf helps to reduce blood pressure and prevent the formation of new blood clots. These herbs work in different ways to help alleviate these conditions, but they all contribute to a healthier cardiovascular system.
intro_image_alt: herbal creams for high blood pressure
